reasoning:
The knowledge cutoff is May 14, 2025, and the resolution date is July 1, 2025, leaving only about 1.5 months for Mahmoud Khalil to be deported. While an immigration judge ruled on April 11, 2025, that he can be deported, this ruling is subject to appeal to the Board of Immigration Appeals (BIA). The BIA process involves procedural timelines (such as obtaining transcripts and filing briefs) that routinely take many months, and a final order of removal is not issued until the appeal is exhausted. 

Furthermore, federal judges in New York and New Jersey have issued stays of removal blocking his deportation while separate litigation (challenging his detention and targeting) proceeds in federal court. On May 7, a federal judge ordered the administration to submit further briefings on its legal arguments, showing that federal litigation is actively ongoing and unlikely to conclude in a matter of weeks. Given the multiple layers of pending litigation and active federal stays of removal, it is exceptionally unlikely that Khalil's legal options will be exhausted and that he will be physically deported before July 1, 2025. I assign a baseline 5% probability accounting for any extraordinary, fast-tracked government action on national security grounds.
